text:
Former Olympic silver medallist James Magnussen has called for “peace” from vegans after receiving online attacks for suggesting plant-based meals reduce performance.

Former Australian Olympic swimmer James Magnussen has revealed he has been targeted by vegans after criticising the “vegan-first mentality” in Paris.

The former Olympic silver medallist has beenvocal about the plant-based mealsbeing served at the athlete village on his podcast, Matty and the Missile in Paris.

Magnussen argued “world-class athletes don’t have vegan diets” and has lambasted the Paris organisers for allegedly running out of meat.

His remarks have sparked a torrent of backlash from the vegan community on social media, where Magnussen said he has been “copping a hammering”.

After publishing an article titled “Eco-friendly, vegan-first mentality is damaging Paris Olympics”, critics were quick to pounce on the former swimming world champion.

“James clearly knows zero about sports nutrition,” one user reacted.

“Do you have any actual evidence to support these claims Mr Magnussen or is this more speculation delivered with conviction?” asked another.

In response to the criticism on Wednesday, Magnussen attempted to diffuse the situation: “To all the vegans out there, ‘peace to you my brethren’.”

“I’m going to sleep easy tonight. You know why? I’ve left no carbon footprint today. I sweated my arse on (a) bus that had no air conditioning.”

He added his co-host Matty Johns had largely avoided the onslaught as he does not have social media.

In his article, Magnussen referenced Usain Bolt, Michael Phelps and Roger Federer and said, “None of these guys are on a vegan diet”.

But many commenters hit back and pointed to elite vegan athletes including Carl Lewis, Lewis Hamilton, Venus Williams and Novak Djokovic.

Some also attacked Magnussen over his participation in the Enhanced Games for which he said he would “juice to the gills” on performance-enhancing drugs to break the 50m freestyle world record.

“This from James Magnussen, who admits he would be willing to ‘dope his brains out’ to swim in the Druggie Games,” retorted one commenter.

“Author Magnussen wants to compete in no holds barred ‘Enhanced’ (doped to the eyeball’s) competition,” wrote another.

Magnussen also spoke earlier in the week on the podcast about how the poor sleeping conditions, notably the cardboard beds, and inadequate dietary provisions at the Olympic Village could cost athletes their chance at a medal.

Magnussen told Johns from Paris: “There will be many athletes across the two weeks of competition who miss out on a medal… because they’re unsettled by this new environment.”

“We haven’t seen this at an Olympic games before. We haven’t had this amount of complaints about a village in Olympics history.”

Magnussen weighed in on the vegan saga as he discussed revelations the village ran out of meat on the first day.

“I joked last night about the amount of vegan options in the village, that they’re running out of meat,” Magnussen said.

“Well, an Australian heavyweight boxer has come out. He wanted lamb chops — (but there’s a) maximum two chops per person. The guy is 6'6", 110(kg) at best.”

Johns joked: “He’s come here as a heavyweight, he’ll go home as a middleweight.”

keywords: plant-based meals,matty and the missile,james magnussen,paris olympics,vegan,the missile
